summary: The test `test_no_truststore_can_install[GitHub]` failed because pip could not download a package from GitHub due to repeated HTTP 502 errors. The test was retried 3 times and failed each time with the same network error, indicating a transient GitHub availability issue.
root_cause: GitHub returned repeated 502 errors when the test attempted to download https://github.com/pypa/pip-test-package/archive/refs/heads/master.zip, causing pip to exhaust its retries and exit with code 1.

evidence.quote: ERROR: Could not install packages due to an OSError: HTTPSConnectionPool(host='github.com', port=443): Max retries exceeded with url: /pypa/pip-test-package/archive/refs/heads/master.zip (Caused by ResponseError('too many 502 error responses'))
evidence.reason: This is the direct error message from pip showing that GitHub returned too many 502 errors, exhausting all retries — a transient network/server-side failure.
The test was retried 3 times and failed each time with the same 502 error, confirming this is a persistent transient network issue rather than a code defect.
The final test result shows the test failed after all retries were exhausted, with the root cause being the network failure to reach GitHub.

evidence.quote: 1 failed, 1080 passed, 44 skipped, 21 xfailed, 2 xpassed, 5 warnings, 3 rerun in 451.05s (0:07:31)
evidence.reason: Only 1 test failed out of 1080 passed, and it was the one making a live network request to GitHub, strongly indicating a transient external network issue rather than a code problem.

suggested_fix: Re-run the CI job, as this is a transient GitHub 502 network failure. Consider mocking the network call or using a local fixture for the test to avoid flakiness from external service availability.
